title: Road Trip from Hell
description: A simple road trip spirals into chaos. Buckle up.

text.mild: {{current}} rented a van for the weekend. The plan: drive to the coast. Simple. Easy. Nothing could go wrong. Everyone piles in. {{next}} calls shotgun.
text.wild: {{current}} 'borrowed' a van from a cousin. The plan: drive to the coast. The van smells like old tacos and regret. Everyone piles in. {{next}} calls shotgun but the seat is stuck reclined all the way back.

text.mild: First order of business: what music are we playing?
text.wild: A fight immediately breaks out over the aux cord. This will define the entire trip. VOTE.

text.mild: Classic road trip jams it is. Everyone's singing along. Life is good. Then the GPS says 'recalculating' for the third time.
text.wild: Classic road trip jams blast through the one working speaker. Everyone's screaming the lyrics. Life is chaotic. Then the GPS starts giving directions in what sounds like Latin.

text.mild: {{current}} has the aux. Sing the chorus of your favorite road trip song right now!
text.wild: {{current}} has the aux and immediately plays their guilty pleasure song. Sing it LOUD and PROUD for the group!

text.mild: You pull into a gas station. It's one of those middle-of-nowhere places with a single pump and a guy who just stares at you.
text.wild: You pull into a gas station that looks like it hasn't been updated since 1974. The attendant has an eye patch and a parrot. The parrot says 'turn back.'

text.mild: {{current}} buys everyone snacks from inside. Cheers to the road trip! Take a sip.
text.wild: {{current}} comes out of the gas station with the most suspicious energy drink you've ever seen. It's glowing. Drink up!

text.mild: {{current}} buys everyone mystery snacks. Eat an imaginary mystery snack and describe its flavor!
text.wild: {{current}} buys a snack with a label in a language nobody recognizes. Describe what it tastes like using only sound effects!

text.mild: Back on the road, you see a sign: 'Scenic Overlook — 2 miles' and another: 'World's Largest Ball of Yarn — 5 miles.' The coast is straight ahead.
text.wild: Back on the road, you see a sign: 'DO NOT ENTER — Scenic Overlook' and another: 'World's Most Cursed Ball of Yarn — 5 miles.' The GPS is recalculating again.

text.mild: The scenic overlook is actually stunning. {{current}} takes a panoramic photo. {{next}} pretends to push someone off the edge. Classic.
text.wild: The scenic overlook is breathtaking. {{current}} is trying to get the perfect selfie and almost falls off the cliff. {{next}} saves them at the last second and will never let them forget it.

text.mild: {{current}}, strike your most dramatic 'standing on a cliff' pose and hold it!
text.wild: {{current}}, do your best impression of someone in a shampoo commercial with the wind in their hair!

text.mild: The World's Largest Ball of Yarn is... actually just a big ball of yarn. But the gift shop is incredible. {{current}} buys a tiny yarn ball keychain.
text.wild: The World's Most Cursed Ball of Yarn is seven stories tall and appears to be breathing. The gift shop sells 'I survived the Yarn' t-shirts. {{current}} buys five.

text.mild: The yarn museum guide offers a private tour. It's getting late. Do you stay?
text.wild: The yarn museum guide, who definitely lives inside the yarn, offers a 'special midnight tour.' It's almost sunset. Do you stay?

text.mild: The tour is actually fascinating! The guide shares homemade lemonade. {{current}}, take a sip to toast this cultural experience.
text.wild: The tour is WILD. The guide tells you the yarn once ate a man. He offers everyone homemade 'yarn juice.' {{current}}, bottoms up.

text.mild: You stay on course like responsible adults. The highway stretches on. {{current}} starts a game of 'I Spy' that gets surprisingly intense.
text.wild: You stay on course. Big mistake. The highway turns into a one-lane road. Then a dirt road. Then what can only be described as a suggestion of a road.

text.mild: The drive is getting long. {{current}}, the navigator, needs a refreshment. Take a sip!
text.wild: {{current}} has been navigating with a paper map upside down for the last hour. Take a penalty drink!

text.mild: {{current}} needs to stay alert as navigator. Do 10 jumping jacks to wake up!
text.wild: {{current}} has been holding the map upside down. Spin around 5 times as punishment!

text.mild: You can see the coast! But there's a fork in the road. One way leads to a busy beach town, the other to a secluded cove.
text.wild: You can SMELL the ocean! But there's a fork in the road. One sign says 'BEACH PARTY' with flashing lights. The other says 'Secret Cove' with a hand-painted skull beneath it.

text.mild: You arrive at the beach town just as the sun starts to set. The boardwalk is alive with music and lights. {{all}} pile out of the van and race to the water. The road trip was worth every wrong turn.
text.wild: You CRASH into the beach town like a hurricane. The van backfires and sets off a car alarm. {{current}} falls out of the van before it stops moving. {{all}} hit the boardwalk and become legends by midnight. Best. Trip. Ever.

text.mild: The secret cove is paradise. Crystal clear water, not another soul in sight. {{all}} spend the evening around a bonfire, telling stories about the drive. This is why you road trip.
text.wild: The secret cove is UNREAL. Turquoise water, perfect sand, and a mysterious cooler that someone left behind full of snacks. {{all}} claim it as your own private beach. {{current}} declares themselves mayor. Nobody argues because they control the snacks.
